platformCapabilityContracts.title: Media library and album discovery
requiredTargetCapabilitySearch: HarmonyOS Cangjie media library album query
HarmonyOS media assets photo album Cangjie API
HarmonyOS Examples media library photo picker Cangjie
platformCapabilityContracts.targetStrategyHint: Use a HarmonyOS media/photo library or picker capability when available; keep repository boundaries so mock data is not the final implementation.

platformCapabilityContracts.title: File access, document permissions, and share URIs
requiredTargetCapabilitySearch: HarmonyOS Cangjie file picker document access
HarmonyOS Cangjie file share URI permissions
platformCapabilityContracts.targetStrategyHint: Replace Android SAF/FileProvider with HarmonyOS file picker, sandbox, or sharing APIs behind a FileAccessService boundary.

platformCapabilityContracts.title: Image viewer gestures and metadata
requiredTargetCapabilitySearch: HarmonyOS Cangjie image component gestures zoom pan
HarmonyOS ArkUI pinch gesture Cangjie image
platformCapabilityContracts.targetStrategyHint: Do not port vendored Android PhotoView line-by-line; implement a target ZoomableImage/MediaViewer contract with gesture or fallback controls.

platformCapabilityContracts.title: Localized resources and formatted strings
requiredTargetCapabilitySearch: HarmonyOS resource qualifiers locale string JSON Cangjie
platformCapabilityContracts.targetStrategyHint: Convert Android string resources and locale groups into HarmonyOS resources or a generated resource adapter; avoid hard-coded English for migrated labels.

platformCapabilityContracts.title: Runtime permissions and protected platform access
platformCapabilityContracts.targetStrategyHint: Map Android runtime permission flows to HarmonyOS permission APIs, preserving denied/empty/retry states.

platformCapabilityContracts.title: Background work, services, receivers, widgets, and notifications
platformCapabilityContracts.targetStrategyHint: Replace Android platform components with HarmonyOS abilities, background tasks, notifications, or documented product-level omissions.
